name: claude-code
description: Claude Code v2.1.88 — Anthropic's AI-powered coding assistant. A React/Ink terminal application with 1,888 TypeScript files implementing an agentic coding workflow.

children.name: src
children.description: Claude Code source root

children.name: assistant
children.description: Manages session history discovery and retrieval, enabling conversation resume and session browsing across past interactions.

children.name: bootstrap
children.description: Holds global mutable state that must be initialized before the application starts, including session IDs, cost counters, token accumulators, and feature flag caches.

children.name: bridge
children.description: Implements the WebSocket-based bridge protocol that connects the CLI to remote control surfaces (mobile, web, IDE extensions), handling registration, reconnection, heartbeats, and permission forwarding.

children.name: buddy
children.description: An experimental companion sprite system that renders animated ASCII characters in the terminal, providing personality-driven notifications and ambient feedback during long operations.

children.name: cli
children.description: Contains the transport and I/O layer for headless and structured output modes, including NDJSON streaming, remote I/O adapters, and CLI exit handling.

children.name: commands
children.description: Registry and lazy-loaded implementations for all slash commands available in the REPL, from /help and /config to /review and /ultraplan.

children.name: add-dir
children.description: Adds an additional working directory to the current session's scope, expanding which files and directories Claude can access beyond the initial project root.

children.name: agents
children.description: Interactive UI for managing agent configurations -- viewing, creating, and editing the agent definitions that AgentTool uses when spawning sub-agents.

children.name: ant-trace
children.description: Internal-only (Anthropic) command for generating trace data from the current session, used for debugging and performance analysis. Stubbed out in external builds.

children.name: autofix-pr
children.description: Internal-only command that automatically fixes issues found in a pull request by running Claude against PR review comments. Stubbed out in external builds.

children.name: backfill-sessions
children.description: Internal-only command that retroactively processes and indexes historical session data. Stubbed out in external builds.

children.name: branch
children.description: Creates or switches to a git worktree branch for isolated experimentation, integrating with the worktree system to provide branch-level session isolation.

children.name: break-cache
children.description: Developer debugging command that invalidates prompt caches by injecting a unique string into the system prompt, forcing a fresh API request without cache hits.

children.name: bridge
children.description: Shows the status of and manages the WebSocket bridge connection used for remote control from mobile, web, and IDE surfaces.

children.name: btw
children.description: A side-question command that lets users ask a quick tangential question without disrupting the main conversation flow, running it through a separate lightweight query.

children.name: bughunter
children.description: Internal-only command for systematic bug hunting in the codebase. Stubbed out in external builds.

children.name: chrome
children.description: Manages the Chrome extension integration, offering setup, permissions configuration, and connection status for the Claude-in-Chrome experience.

children.name: clear
children.description: Clears the current conversation history and optionally purges various caches (session, file state, MCP connections) to start fresh.

children.name: color
children.description: Changes the terminal color output settings, toggling between color modes for accessibility or preference.

children.name: compact
children.description: Triggers manual context compaction, summarizing the conversation so far to free up token budget while preserving key information for continued work.

children.name: config
children.description: Opens an interactive settings editor for viewing and modifying Claude Code configuration at project or global scope, with aliases for /settings.

children.name: context
children.description: Displays the current context window contents including system prompt, CLAUDE.md files, conversation messages, and token usage breakdown, useful for understanding what the model sees.

children.name: copy
children.description: Copies the last assistant response or a specified conversation segment to the system clipboard for easy sharing.

children.name: cost
children.description: Displays a detailed breakdown of API token usage and estimated costs for the current session, including per-model and cache statistics.

children.name: ctx_viz
children.description: Internal-only context visualization command for debugging prompt composition. Stubbed out in external builds.

children.name: debug-tool-call
children.description: Internal-only debugging command for inspecting tool call payloads and execution details. Stubbed out in external builds.

children.name: desktop
children.description: Hands off the current session to the Claude Desktop application, enabling continuation of the conversation in a richer GUI environment.

children.name: diff
children.description: Shows a diff view of all file changes made during the current conversation, rendered as a scrollable dialog with syntax highlighting.

children.name: doctor
children.description: Runs diagnostic checks on the Claude Code installation, verifying API connectivity, authentication status, tool availability, and configuration validity.

children.name: effort
children.description: Adjusts the reasoning effort level for subsequent model responses, controlling the depth of thinking the model applies to each turn.

children.name: env
children.description: Internal-only command for inspecting environment variables and runtime configuration. Stubbed out in external builds.

children.name: exit
children.description: Gracefully exits the Claude Code session, running cleanup handlers, saving session state, and displaying the cost summary.

children.name: export
children.description: Exports the current conversation to a file in various formats (plain text, markdown, JSON), providing a portable record of the session.

children.name: extra-usage
children.description: Manages overage billing settings for Claude.ai subscribers, allowing users to enable or configure pay-per-use beyond their plan's included limits.

children.name: fast
children.description: Toggles fast mode which routes queries to a smaller, faster model (like Haiku) for quick interactions where full Opus-level reasoning isn't needed.

children.name: feedback
children.description: Submits user feedback about Claude Code directly to the development team, collecting structured reports with optional session context.

children.name: files
children.description: Lists all files that have been read or modified during the current session, providing an audit trail of the model's filesystem interactions.

children.name: good-claude
children.description: Internal-only positive feedback command used by Anthropic employees to flag exemplary model behavior for training data. Stubbed out in external builds.

children.name: heapdump
children.description: Developer debugging command that dumps the JavaScript heap to disk for memory analysis and leak investigation.

children.name: help
children.description: Displays the full list of available slash commands with descriptions, organized by category, and shows keyboard shortcuts.

children.name: hooks
children.description: Interactive viewer for the configured lifecycle hooks, showing which events have registered hooks and their current status.

children.name: ide
children.description: Manages IDE integration settings and displays the connection status for supported editors (VS Code, JetBrains), enabling bidirectional communication between Claude Code and the IDE.

children.name: install-github-app
children.description: Multi-step wizard for installing the Claude GitHub App on a repository, configuring API keys, checking existing secrets, and creating the GitHub Actions integration.

children.name: install-slack-app
children.description: Installs the Claude Slack App integration, connecting Claude Code capabilities to a Slack workspace.

children.name: issue
children.description: Internal-only command for creating GitHub issues from within a session. Stubbed out in external builds.

children.name: keybindings
children.description: Editor for viewing and customizing keyboard shortcut mappings, with validation against reserved shortcuts and persistence to ~/.claude/keybindings.json.

children.name: login
children.description: Initiates the OAuth authentication flow for Claude.ai or API key entry, handling the PKCE flow, device code grant, and credential storage.

children.name: logout
children.description: Clears stored authentication credentials and OAuth tokens, signing the user out of their current identity.

children.name: mcp
children.description: Manages Model Context Protocol server connections, providing sub-commands for adding, removing, configuring, and monitoring MCP servers attached to the session.

children.name: memory
children.description: Views and edits the persistent memory files (CLAUDE.md and memory directory entries) that carry context across sessions.

children.name: mobile
children.description: Displays a QR code and URL for connecting to the current session from a mobile device through the remote bridge.

children.name: mock-limits
children.description: Internal-only testing command for simulating rate limit scenarios with mock API response headers. Stubbed out in external builds.

children.name: model
children.description: Switches the active model for subsequent queries, with validation of available models and display of the current selection.

children.name: oauth-refresh
children.description: Internal-only command for manually refreshing OAuth tokens. Stubbed out in external builds.

children.name: onboarding
children.description: Internal-only command for replaying the onboarding experience. Stubbed out in external builds.

children.name: output-style
children.description: Deprecated command (now in /config) that previously let users change Claude's response formatting style.

children.name: passes
children.description: Manages Claude Code passes and referral rewards for Claude.ai subscribers, showing eligibility status and referral links.

children.name: perf-issue
children.description: Internal-only command for reporting performance issues with diagnostic data. Stubbed out in external builds.

children.name: permissions
children.description: Interactive manager for tool permission rules, allowing users to view, add, and remove allow/deny rules that control which tools the model can use without asking.

children.name: plan
children.description: Enters plan mode or displays the current session plan, where the model outlines its approach in a structured plan file before executing changes.

children.name: plugin
children.description: Full-featured plugin management interface with marketplace browsing, installation, trust verification, settings configuration, and update management.

children.name: pr_comments
children.description: Fetches and displays comments from a GitHub pull request, now implemented as a plugin redirect via the createMovedToPluginCommand pattern.

children.name: privacy-settings
children.description: Views and updates privacy settings for consumer subscribers, controlling data retention and telemetry preferences.

children.name: rate-limit-options
children.description: Shows available options when a rate limit is reached for Claude.ai subscribers, including upgrade paths and wait time estimates.

children.name: release-notes
children.description: Displays the release notes for the current and recent versions of Claude Code, highlighting new features and changes.

children.name: reload-plugins
children.description: Hot-reloads plugin configurations and applies pending changes to the running session without requiring a restart.

children.name: remote-env
children.description: Configures the default remote environment settings for teleported sessions, controlling which cloud environment is used for Claude Code on the web.

children.name: remote-setup
children.description: Multi-step wizard for setting up Claude Code on the web, including GitHub account connection and remote session configuration.

children.name: rename
children.description: Renames the current conversation session, either with a user-provided name or by auto-generating one from the conversation content.

children.name: reset-limits
children.description: Internal-only command for resetting rate limit counters during testing. Stubbed out in external builds.

children.name: resume
children.description: Presents a picker for browsing and resuming previous conversation sessions, with search, session metadata display, and continuation from where you left off.

children.name: review
children.description: Runs an AI-powered code review of a GitHub PR, analyzing the diff for quality issues, risks, and improvement suggestions. Supports both local and web-based (ultrareview) modes.

children.name: rewind
children.description: Restores files and/or conversation state to a previous checkpoint, using file history snapshots to undo changes made during the session.

children.name: sandbox-toggle
children.description: Toggles the filesystem sandbox on or off and configures auto-allow-bash-if-sandboxed behavior, giving users control over the security boundary.

children.name: session
children.description: Displays the current remote session URL and QR code when running in remote mode, enabling easy connection from other devices.

children.name: share
children.description: Internal-only command for sharing session transcripts. Stubbed out in external builds.

children.name: skills
children.description: Lists all available skills (from SKILL.md files and plugins) with their descriptions, showing what specialized capabilities can be invoked.

children.name: stats
children.description: Shows personal Claude Code usage statistics and activity metrics, including session counts, token usage history, and usage patterns.

children.name: status
children.description: Displays comprehensive system status including version, active model, account type, API connectivity, MCP server health, and tool availability.

children.name: stickers
children.description: A fun Easter egg command that lets users order physical Claude Code stickers.

children.name: summary
children.description: Internal-only command for generating session summaries. Stubbed out in external builds.

children.name: tag
children.description: Internal-only (Anthropic) command for adding searchable tags to sessions, enabling organization and retrieval of sessions by topic or project.

children.name: tasks
children.description: Lists and manages background tasks (sub-agents, shell processes, dreams) with status display, output viewing, and task termination controls.

children.name: teleport
children.description: Internal-only command for teleporting a session to a remote cloud environment. Stubbed out in external builds.

children.name: terminalSetup
children.description: Guides users through terminal configuration for optimal Claude Code experience, detecting terminal capabilities (CSI u, Kitty protocol) and suggesting settings.

children.name: theme
children.description: Interactive theme picker for switching between visual themes that control colors, borders, and styling across the entire terminal UI.

children.name: thinkback
children.description: A year-in-review experience that generates a personalized retrospective of your Claude Code usage, including statistics, highlights, and animated playback.

children.name: thinkback-play
children.description: Hidden companion to /think-back that plays the generated retrospective animation after the thinkback skill finishes producing it.

children.name: upgrade
children.description: Presents Claude.ai subscribers with upgrade options for higher-tier plans that offer increased rate limits and more access to Opus.

children.name: usage
children.description: Shows current plan usage limits and consumption for Claude.ai subscribers, including remaining quota and reset timing.

children.name: vim
children.description: Toggles between Vim and normal editing modes in the REPL input, persisting the preference across sessions.

children.name: voice
children.description: Toggles voice input mode on or off, enabling push-to-talk audio transcription through the voice service.

children.name: components
children.description: React (Ink) UI components that compose the terminal interface, including dialogs, diffs, tool progress indicators, markdown rendering, permission prompts, and the design system primitives.

children.name: hooks
children.description: React hooks that wire together the REPL's runtime behavior -- from input handling and keybindings to IDE integration, voice mode, background task navigation, and notification scheduling.

children.name: utils
children.description: The largest module -- a broad utility library covering git operations, file handling, permissions, model configuration, sandboxing, theming, authentication, process management, and dozens of other cross-cutting concerns.

children.name: skills
children.description: The skill system that discovers and loads SKILL.md files from project directories and bundled sources, builds tool-like wrappers for MCP-provided skills, and manages the skill registry.

children.name: tasks
children.description: Task type implementations for background work: local shell commands, local agent subprocesses, remote agent sessions, in-process teammates, dream tasks, and workflow script execution.

children.name: constants
children.description: Compile-time and runtime constants covering API limits, XML tags, key codes, figure characters, OAuth endpoints, product URLs, prompt templates, and error identifiers.

children.name: context
children.description: React context providers for cross-cutting concerns like voice state, modal overlays, notification queues, FPS metrics, mailbox messaging, and queued message delivery.

children.name: coordinator
children.description: Implements the coordinator/orchestrator mode for multi-agent sessions, defining the tool whitelist and behavioral constraints that distinguish a coordinator agent from a worker agent.

children.name: entrypoints
children.description: The initialization entry points for all interface modes: interactive CLI, headless SDK, and MCP server, including the shared init sequence and sandbox type definitions.

children.name: hooks
children.description: React hooks that wire together the REPL's runtime behavior -- from input handling and keybindings to IDE integration, voice mode, background task navigation, and notification scheduling.

children.name: ink
children.description: A vendored and extended fork of the Ink terminal React renderer, adding synchronized output, virtual scrolling, keyboard event handling, terminal capability detection, and the core Box/Text primitives.

children.name: components
children.description: React (Ink) UI components that compose the terminal interface, including dialogs, diffs, tool progress indicators, markdown rendering, permission prompts, and the design system primitives.

children.name: hooks
children.description: React hooks that wire together the REPL's runtime behavior -- from input handling and keybindings to IDE integration, voice mode, background task navigation, and notification scheduling.

children.name: keybindings
children.description: The keybinding system that loads user-customizable key mappings, resolves chord sequences, matches keyboard events to actions, and enforces reserved shortcut constraints.

children.name: memdir
children.description: The memory directory system that discovers, loads, scores, and injects CLAUDE.md-style memory files from project and user directories, including team memory paths and age-based relevance filtering.

children.name: migrations
children.description: One-shot data migrations that run on startup to evolve persisted config state, such as renaming model identifiers, moving settings between config scopes, and resetting stale feature opt-ins.

children.name: moreright
children.description: An internal-only hook stub for Anthropic employees that intercepts conversation turns to inject additional context or side-channel processing before and after queries.

children.name: native-ts
children.description: Pure TypeScript reimplementations of performance-critical native modules (file indexing, syntax-highlighted color diffs, yoga layout), providing the same API without requiring Rust NAPI binaries.

children.name: outputStyles
children.description: Loads user-defined output style configurations from markdown files in .claude/output-styles directories, allowing customization of Claude's response formatting through frontmatter-driven templates.

children.name: plugins
children.description: The plugin system that discovers, loads, validates, and manages third-party extensions, including the built-in plugin registry and the bundled plugin loader.

children.name: query
children.description: Configuration and dependency injection for the query pipeline, including token budget management, stop hooks, and query-level config overrides that feed into QueryEngine.

children.name: remote
children.description: Manages remote session connectivity including the WebSocket transport for teleported sessions, SDK message adaptation, and permission bridging between local and remote environments.

children.name: schemas
children.description: Zod validation schemas for user-facing configuration formats, particularly the hooks configuration schema that enforces structure on lifecycle event definitions.

children.name: screens
children.description: Top-level screen components that represent full application views: the main REPL, the conversation resume picker, and the diagnostic doctor screen.

children.name: server
children.description: The direct-connect server module that enables WebSocket-based control of a Claude Code session from external clients, managing session creation and bidirectional message routing.

children.name: services
children.description: Backend service modules covering API communication, analytics, compaction, MCP server management, OAuth, plugin operations, rate limiting, voice processing, and various background processes.

children.name: AgentSummary
children.description: Generates concise summaries of agent sub-task results for injection back into the parent conversation, compressing lengthy agent outputs into actionable bullet points.

children.name: MagicDocs
children.description: Automatically generates and maintains project documentation by analyzing codebase structure, using prompt templates to produce contextually relevant documentation content.

children.name: PromptSuggestion
children.description: Generates contextual prompt suggestions and speculative completions based on conversation history and project state, powering the typeahead and suggestion UI.

children.name: SessionMemory
children.description: Persists and retrieves cross-session memory by extracting key facts, decisions, and patterns from conversations, enabling continuity between sessions through intelligent memory prompts.

children.name: analytics
children.description: Telemetry and analytics infrastructure including event logging, Datadog integration, GrowthBook feature flag evaluation, and first-party event collection with privacy controls.

children.name: api
children.description: The API client layer handling all communication with the Anthropic API, including streaming, retry logic, error categorization, prompt dumping, token counting, client bootstrapping, and admin requests.

children.name: autoDream
children.description: Background autonomous agent that periodically wakes up to perform maintenance tasks (memory consolidation, code cleanup) when the session is idle, with configurable scheduling and consolidation locking.

children.name: compact
children.description: Context compaction system that summarizes conversation history to fit within token limits, including auto-compaction triggers, reactive compaction, API-side micro-compaction, and compaction boundary management.

children.name: extractMemories
children.description: Extracts persistent memories from conversation turns using model-generated summaries, identifying important facts, preferences, and decisions worth remembering across sessions.

children.name: lsp
children.description: Language Server Protocol client infrastructure that manages LSP server instances, diagnostic registries, and server lifecycle, providing code intelligence capabilities to the LSPTool.

children.name: mcp
children.description: The Model Context Protocol implementation including connection management, server discovery, transport layers (stdio, SSE, in-process), authentication, channel allowlisting, and the official registry prefetch.

children.name: oauth
children.description: OAuth 2.0 client implementation supporting PKCE authorization code flow and device code grants, with crypto helpers, token storage, profile retrieval, and the local auth-code listener server.

children.name: plugins
children.description: Plugin management infrastructure for installing, updating, validating, and executing third-party plugins, including marketplace integration and CLI command generation from plugin metadata.

children.name: policyLimits
children.description: Enterprise policy enforcement layer that loads and evaluates organization-level restrictions (feature allowlists, tool blocklists) applied by administrators through remote configuration.

children.name: remoteManagedSettings
children.description: Fetches and applies remotely-managed configuration from an organization's admin console, syncing server-side settings overrides and security policies to the local client.

children.name: settingsSync
children.description: Synchronizes user settings between the local Claude Code installation and the cloud, ensuring preference consistency across devices and sessions.

children.name: teamMemorySync
children.description: Synchronizes team-shared memory files across team members, with secret scanning and a guard system that prevents sensitive content from being committed to shared memory.

children.name: tips
children.description: A tips system that schedules and displays contextual usage tips based on user activity history, with a registry of available tips and a scheduler that determines display timing.

children.name: toolUseSummary
children.description: Generates human-readable summaries of tool execution results, compressing verbose tool outputs into concise descriptions for the conversation history.

children.name: tools
children.description: The tool execution orchestration layer that handles streaming tool execution, lifecycle hooks (pre/post execution), tool result processing, and the coordination between QueryEngine and individual tool implementations.

children.name: skills
children.description: The skill system that discovers and loads SKILL.md files from project directories and bundled sources, builds tool-like wrappers for MCP-provided skills, and manages the skill registry.

children.name: state
children.description: The centralized application state store (AppState) with React context integration, state selectors, teammate view helpers, and side-effect handlers triggered by state transitions.

children.name: tasks
children.description: Task type implementations for background work: local shell commands, local agent subprocesses, remote agent sessions, in-process teammates, dream tasks, and workflow script execution.

children.name: tools
children.description: All 40+ tool implementations that Claude can invoke, from file operations (Read/Write/Edit) and search (Glob/Grep) to agent spawning, MCP integration, task management, and web fetching.

children.name: AgentTool
children.description: Spawns autonomous sub-agents that run in isolated conversation contexts with their own system prompts and tool permissions, enabling parallel task decomposition and specialized agent types (coder, reviewer, planner).

children.name: AskUserQuestionTool
children.description: Allows the model to explicitly pause and ask the user a question when it needs clarification, surfacing a structured prompt in the terminal rather than guessing.

children.name: BashTool
children.description: Executes shell commands in a managed subprocess with timeout enforcement, sandboxing integration, output truncation, and streaming progress updates back to the conversation.

children.name: BriefTool
children.description: A synthetic tool that switches the model into a concise response mode, reducing verbosity for the remainder of the conversation when the user prefers terse output.

children.name: ConfigTool
children.description: Reads and writes Claude Code configuration values programmatically, giving the model the ability to adjust settings (like output style or model) during a session.

children.name: EnterPlanModeTool
children.description: Transitions the session into plan mode where the model outlines steps before executing, restricting the available tool set to read-only operations until the user approves the plan.

children.name: EnterWorktreeTool
children.description: Creates or switches to a git worktree for isolated experimentation, letting the model make changes on a separate branch without affecting the user's working directory.

children.name: ExitPlanModeTool
children.description: Transitions the session out of plan mode back to normal execution mode, re-enabling the full tool set after the planning phase is complete.

children.name: ExitWorktreeTool
children.description: Leaves the current git worktree and returns to the original working directory, optionally merging or discarding the worktree's changes.

children.name: FileEditTool
children.description: Performs surgical string-replacement edits on files using an old_string/new_string pattern, with uniqueness validation and optional replace-all semantics for safe, reviewable modifications.

children.name: FileReadTool
children.description: Reads file contents with optional line-range selection, supporting text files, images (returned as base64), PDFs (page-range extraction), and Jupyter notebooks.

children.name: FileWriteTool
children.description: Writes complete file contents to disk, creating parent directories as needed, with guards against overwriting files that haven't been read first.

children.name: GlobTool
children.description: Performs fast file pattern matching across the project using glob syntax, returning sorted results for file discovery without reading file contents.

children.name: GrepTool
children.description: Searches file contents using ripgrep-powered regex with support for context lines, file type filtering, glob-based path filtering, and multiple output modes.

children.name: LSPTool
children.description: Queries connected Language Server Protocol servers for type definitions, references, diagnostics, and symbol information, giving the model IDE-level code intelligence.

children.name: ListMcpResourcesTool
children.description: Lists available resources from connected MCP servers, enabling the model to discover what data sources (files, databases, APIs) are accessible through the MCP protocol.

children.name: MCPTool
children.description: A dynamic tool wrapper that forwards invocations to tools provided by connected MCP servers, bridging external tool ecosystems into Claude's native tool interface.

children.name: McpAuthTool
children.description: Handles OAuth authentication flows for MCP servers that require credentials, managing token acquisition and storage for authenticated MCP connections.

children.name: NotebookEditTool
children.description: Edits Jupyter notebook cells by index, supporting cell content replacement, insertion, and deletion while preserving notebook metadata and structure.

children.name: PowerShellTool
children.description: Executes PowerShell commands on Windows systems, providing the Windows-native shell equivalent of BashTool with appropriate encoding and execution policy handling.

children.name: REPLTool
children.description: An internal-only tool that executes code in a persistent REPL session (Python, Node, etc.), maintaining state across invocations for iterative development and data exploration.

children.name: ReadMcpResourceTool
children.description: Reads the contents of a specific MCP resource by URI, returning the data for the model to process -- the read counterpart to ListMcpResourcesTool.

children.name: RemoteTriggerTool
children.description: Creates remote webhook triggers that can start new agent sessions when external events occur, enabling event-driven automation workflows.

children.name: ScheduleCronTool
children.description: Manages cron-style scheduled tasks that run agent sessions on a recurring schedule, with sub-tools for creating, listing, and deleting cron entries.

children.name: SendMessageTool
children.description: Sends messages to other agents in a multi-agent swarm, enabling inter-agent communication and coordination during parallel task execution.

children.name: SkillTool
children.description: Invokes named skills (loaded from SKILL.md files) that inject specialized prompts, tool restrictions, and behavioral instructions into a sub-agent context.

children.name: SleepTool
children.description: Pauses the agent for a specified duration, used in proactive/autonomous agent modes where the agent needs to wait for external events or schedule future work.

children.name: SyntheticOutputTool
children.description: An invisible tool that forces the model to produce structured output in a specific format, used internally to extract structured data from model responses without user-visible tool calls.

children.name: TaskCreateTool
children.description: Creates background tasks (shell commands or agent sessions) that run concurrently with the main conversation, returning a task ID for later status checking.

children.name: TaskGetTool
children.description: Retrieves the current status and recent output of a background task by its ID, letting the model check on long-running operations.

children.name: TaskListTool
children.description: Lists all active and recently completed background tasks with their statuses, providing an overview of concurrent work.

children.name: TaskOutputTool
children.description: Fetches the full accumulated output of a background task, useful for reading complete results after a task finishes.

children.name: TaskStopTool
children.description: Terminates a running background task by sending it an abort signal, cleaning up resources and marking the task as killed.

children.name: TaskUpdateTool
children.description: Sends a message or instruction to a running background agent task, enabling mid-flight guidance and course correction of autonomous sub-tasks.

children.name: TeamCreateTool
children.description: Spawns a persistent teammate agent that runs as an in-process concurrent session, participating in the multi-agent swarm with its own conversation context.

children.name: TeamDeleteTool
children.description: Removes a teammate agent from the active swarm, terminating its session and cleaning up its resources.

children.name: TodoWriteTool
children.description: Writes and manages a structured TODO list that persists across turns, giving the model a scratchpad for tracking multi-step task progress.

children.name: ToolSearchTool
children.description: Searches the deferred tool registry by keyword or name to discover available tools that aren't loaded by default, enabling on-demand tool activation.

children.name: WebFetchTool
children.description: Fetches web page content by URL, converting HTML to readable text with optional JavaScript rendering, giving the model access to documentation and web resources.

children.name: WebSearchTool
children.description: Performs web searches and returns summarized results, enabling the model to find current information beyond its training data.

children.name: shared
children.description: Shared utilities used across multiple tools, including git operation tracking for attribution and the multi-agent spawn helper that standardizes sub-agent creation.

children.name: testing
children.description: Test-only tool implementations (like TestingPermissionTool) used in the test suite to exercise the permission system without depending on real tools.

children.name: types
children.description: Shared TypeScript type definitions for messages, permissions, commands, hooks, plugin shapes, text input state, log formats, and generated API types.

children.name: upstreamproxy
children.description: Container-side networking module for Claude Code on the web (CCR) sessions that sets up a CONNECT-to-WebSocket relay proxy, injects CA certificates, and manages session token security.

children.name: utils
children.description: The largest module -- a broad utility library covering git operations, file handling, permissions, model configuration, sandboxing, theming, authentication, process management, and dozens of other cross-cutting concerns.

children.name: remote
children.description: Manages remote session connectivity including the WebSocket transport for teleported sessions, SDK message adaptation, and permission bridging between local and remote environments.

children.name: hooks
children.description: React hooks that wire together the REPL's runtime behavior -- from input handling and keybindings to IDE integration, voice mode, background task navigation, and notification scheduling.

children.name: plugins
children.description: The plugin system that discovers, loads, validates, and manages third-party extensions, including the built-in plugin registry and the bundled plugin loader.

children.name: skills
children.description: The skill system that discovers and loads SKILL.md files from project directories and bundled sources, builds tool-like wrappers for MCP-provided skills, and manages the skill registry.

children.name: vim
children.description: Vim emulation layer that implements motions, operators, text objects, and mode transitions for vim-style editing in the REPL input.

children.name: voice
children.description: Voice mode feature-gating module that checks both compile-time feature flags and runtime GrowthBook kill-switches to determine whether voice input should be available.

children.name: vendor
children.description: Native module stubs — platform-specific Rust/C++ bindings compiled via napi-rs. These provide audio capture, image processing, keyboard modifier detection, and URL handling capabilities that cannot be implemented in pure TypeScript.

children.name: audio-capture-src
children.description: Native audio capture bindings for voice input mode. Interfaces with platform audio APIs (CoreAudio on macOS, ALSA on Linux) via napi-rs.

children.name: image-processor-src
children.description: Native image processing for screenshots and visual input. Handles format conversion and resizing before sending images to the Claude API.

children.name: modifiers-napi-src
children.description: Native keyboard modifier key detection via napi-rs. Detects Shift/Ctrl/Alt/Meta key states for permission mode cycling (Shift+Tab) and other keyboard shortcuts.

children.name: url-handler-src
children.description: Native URL handler for OAuth callback reception. Registers as a custom protocol handler (claude://) to receive authentication callbacks from the browser.
